impact:
V1L's WET path (BLEND=1.00, so upstream of BLEND and contaminating everything downstream) is LF-deficient by -12.9dB mean below 100Hz: -21.8dB @25Hz, -12.6 @40, -8.8 @63, -6.4 @100, tapering to -1.9 @400.

THE NETLIST PREDICTED THIS AND NAMED THE GATE. netlists.md L5d flags C10/R14 as "the least-certain read on this revision" with the self-validation gate: "if the derived V1L wet-path LF misses §1, re-crop this node first". src/dsp/V1LateStages.h:174-180 carries the same warning inline. C10=10n into R14=100k = a 159.2 Hz single-pole highpass in the WET path of a BASS pedal — implausible on its face.

EVIDENCE (4-way triangulation from CAPTURES):
1. Shape: the measured deficit tracks a single-pole HP at 159Hz within ~1dB over 40-127Hz.
2. Revision-exclusivity: the wet make-up buffer carrying C10/R14 is V1L-ONLY, and the LF deficit is V1L-ONLY (V1E/V2 clean).
3. Fit: scanning E12 values with the mean removed drops shape residual RMS 4.27dB (10n) -> 0.58dB, saturating at >=100n (can't pin exact value — corner falls below measured band).
4. The gate's own precondition (V1L wet-path LF misses FR §1) is met per captures.

*** 2026-07-16 SPICE CROSS-CHECK (§1) GIVES A CONFLICTING SIGNAL — DO NOT FIX C10 WITHOUT RESOLVING THIS FIRST ***
Rendered the plugin directly (V1L, PRESENCE=0/DRIVE=0/BLEND=1, no capture involved — analysis/spice_target_check.py) and measured the LF-edge (~25Hz) relative to the low-bump peak (~100Hz): plugin reads -4.7dB. SPICE §1 target for V1 Late: LF edge (~20-30Hz) "~-10dB" relative to the curve. That is the OPPOSITE direction from the capture-based finding: SPICE says the plugin's LF rolloff, judged against its OWN curve shape, is already too SHALLOW (under-attenuated), while the captures say the plugin is too attenuated vs the real pedal. Raising C10 (the capture-motivated fix) would make the plugin's LF-edge-to-bump delta SHALLOWER STILL — moving it further from the SPICE target.

CAVEAT ON THIS NEW SIGNAL: reference-fr-targets.md's own stated convention is ambiguous about what "0dB" anchors the §1 curve ("each sim is normalised its own way" — stated precisely for tone controls and PRESENCE/DRIVE curves, NOT stated for §1's combined character curve). My LF-edge-relative-to-low-bump-peak read is a reasonable guess at matching the table's implied convention but is UNVERIFIED — an earlier version of this exact script picked a bad reference band (200-400Hz, which sits on the ~430Hz bridged-T's shoulder) and produced an inflated, wrong result on the §8 checkpoints before being caught and fixed to a reference-band-free peak-to-valley metric; §1's LF-edge-to-bump metric has the same class of risk and has NOT been similarly cross-checked.

Also note POSITIVE corroboration from the same session: the twin-T notch's FREQUENCY (721-803Hz plugin vs 700-750Hz SPICE, all 3 checkpoints) and DEPTH relative to its neighbouring bumps (within ~1.4-2.5dB at moderate/high DRIVE+PRESENCE) match SPICE well — so the notch component values are independently corroborated as correct; it is specifically the very-low-frequency (<~100Hz) region under dispute.

REVISED NEXT STEP: do not treat "raise C10" as settled. First reconcile the SPICE §1 LF-edge reading against the capture-based reading — likely by testing whether the LF deficit's SIGN/MAGNITUDE the captures show is itself an artifact of what capture was used (recall kOutputMakeup[1] was ALSO fitted on this same capture, so gain-matching per-capture may not have removed all of a level confound at LF specifically — worth checking whether other V1L captures at BL=1.00 would exist to cross-validate, though only one V1L BL=1.00 capture exists in the current matrix). If no second wet-path V1L capture is available (per capture-availability limits, N-001/N-002), re-crop the schematic node per the original gate AND independently re-verify the §1 SPICE LF figure's normalization before changing C10.

Reproduce: python3.11 analysis/cascade_analysis.py (section A); python3.11 analysis/spice_target_check.py --rev V1L --os 8.
title: V1L wet-path LF deficit −12.9 dB: the flagged C10/R14 netlist gate has FIRED (C10 10n reads as a 159 Hz HP)
